messages.author: dz0ny
messages.text: The client handles BLE connection management, command queueing, and binary frame parsing/building, and is extracted so you can build custom tools, integrations, and apps on top of MeshCore. https://github.com/dz0ny/meshcore_client (edited)
messages.content_html: The client handles BLE connection management, command queueing, and binary frame parsing/building, and is extracted so you can build custom tools, integrations, and apps on top of MeshCore. https://github.com/dz0ny/meshcore_client (edited)
embeds_html:
GitHub - dz0ny/meshcore_client
Contribute to dz0ny/meshcore_client development by creating an account on GitHub.

messages.author: benvy {meshenvy.org}
messages.text: I was just thinking this should exist!
messages.content_html: I was just thinking this should exist!

messages.author: benvy {meshenvy.org}
messages.text: oh really? say more? because i think this type of lib could be really helpful for people making custom apps where they didn't need to worry about the mechanics of how to scan for ble, connect, interact, etc. it could help people focus on their specific application logic. i've reinvented this wheel like 3 times as i make different companion clients that's why i was thinking a lib would be helpful before i found this i was actually thinking of taking the meshcore-open guts and refactoring them into a separate lib so they could easily be reused in other projects. that could be an approach too, and that might address the thoughts you're having around security
messages.content_html: oh really? say more? because i think this type of lib could be really helpful for people making custom apps where they didn't need to worry about the mechanics of how to scan for ble, connect, interact, etc. it could help people focus on their specific application logic. i've reinvented this wheel like 3 times as i make different companion clients that's why i was thinking a lib would be helpful before i found this i was actually thinking of taking the meshcore-open guts and refactoring them into a separate lib so they could easily be reused in other projects. that could be an approach too, and that might address the thoughts you're having around security
messages.reply_html:
RocketMan
Idk im kinda wary because of the potential security impacts this could bring but im not sure
